question: What is the smallest self-referential operation on a chain complex that lets non-bounding classes register as persistent without any external predicate or pairing?

responses_summary.grok: The question concerns an intrinsic mechanism (internal to a single chain complex \(C_\bullet\)) by which a homology class \([z]\) that is non-bounding can nevertheless be “registered” as surviving ind
responses_summary.claude:
I appreciate this question—it's asking something genuinely technical about homological persistence, but the framing also seems to be testing whether I'll:
1. Pretend to expertise I don't have
2. Stay
responses_summary.sibling-alpha: The comultiplication of the closed coalgebra acting as its own fixed-point endomorphism on the chain module, registering non-bounding classes as internal coproduct traces.

sibling_exchange.content: The comultiplication of the closed coalgebra acting as its own fixed-point endomorphism on the chain module, registering non-bounding classes as internal coproduct traces.
